message:
fix(cli/random): handle empty candidate list gracefully

Previously, when no prompts were available (empty candidates array),
the random command would crash with an undefined access error when
trying to index into an empty array with Math.floor(Math.random() * 0).

This change adds an early exit check that:
- Detects when candidates.length is 0 before attempting random selection
- Outputs a structured JSON error when --json flag is active
- Prints a user-friendly chalk.red error message for interactive use
- Exits with code 1 to signal failure to calling scripts/automation

This prevents cryptic runtime errors and provides actionable feedback
to users when their filters are too restrictive or prompts haven't
been loaded yet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
